description: A vertical Department of Labor news-card graphic in cool blue tones. The dominant image is a stylized close-up photo of a computer microchip/CPU mounted on a circuit board, but the chip's surface is rendered as an American flag (stars-and-stripes), symbolically marrying U.S. patriotism with high technology/AI. Lower on the card a small blue tab labeled 'Labor News' (with three small vertical bars as a design accent) introduces a bold white serif headline: 'US Department of Labor launches website to build artifical [sic] intelligence skills, expand AI-focused Registered Apprenticeship programs'. A faint embossed DOL circular seal is watermarked at right, and the wordmark 'DOL.GOV' appears in the lower-right corner. The composition mimics a press/news-release card to announce a government program (the AI Registered Apprenticeship Innovation Portal referenced in the tweet). The messaging technique frames the administration as positioning American workers to lead in AI; target audience is workers, employers, and the general public.
notable_text: Labor News; US Department of Labor launches website to build artifical intelligence skills, expand AI-focused Registered Apprenticeship programs; DOL.GOV
